history: The client has a history of working overseas and building a website related to that work. He repeatedly starts new projects with enthusiasm but abandons them when difficulties arise, a pattern that parallels his current struggles. He has experienced family-related difficulties, particularly involving his father, and engaged in a forgiveness process by writing a personal letter. He also reports ongoing issues with medication management, ADHD symptoms, and financial stressors related to paying for prescriptions out of pocket.
intermediate_belief: If I encounter difficulties or obstacles, it means I'm not capable and should give up before I fail. When things don't go as planned, it's safer to avoid them rather than risk proving that I'm not good enough. If I can't manage everything perfectly, others will see me as unreliable or inadequate.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I keep failing to overcome challenges, it proves that nothing will ever change for me and there's no point in trying. When I reflect on my past achievements but can't match them now, it means I've lost my ability and things will only get worse.
coping_strategies: He tends to avoid or abandon projects when faced with obstacles, withdrawing from tasks that feel overwhelming. He seeks external solutions, such as adjusting medication or considering therapy, but hesitates to assert himself directly with others (e.g., pharmacies) due to fear of being perceived negatively. He relies on caffeine and overcompensation to manage fatigue, which may perpetuate cycles of avoidance and low motivation.
situation: Reflecting on past achievements alongside current challenges.
auto_thought: I used to accomplish things, but now I can't handle even simple problems. This just proves I'm not capable anymore.
behavior: He dwells on his past successes and current difficulties, avoids starting or continuing challenging tasks, and withdraws from activities that feel overwhelming. He may ruminate about his failures and hesitate to seek direct help or clarification from others.
